programs.name: Health First Colorado Buy-In (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These Medicare Savings Programs help pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income seniors. In Colorado, administered as Health First Colorado Buy-In programs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ with Medicare; QMB (income ≤100% FPL), SLMB (≤120% FPL), QI (≤135% FPL); asset limits apply.
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premiums/cost-sharing ($0 copays); SLMB: Part B premium (~$174.70/month); QI: Partial premium assistance.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers (e.g., Home Care Allowance)
programs.what_it_does: These waivers provide home-based services like personal care, respite, home modifications, and adult day care to help seniors avoid institutionalization. The Home Care Allowance (HCA) specifically pays for personal care, respite, and supplies.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ with income ≤ 300% Federal Benefit Rate (~$3,200/month individual), assets ≤ $2,000.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,500/month for HCA; other waivers vary by services approved.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health, transportation, and home care to prevent nursing home placement. In Colorado, it's administered through select providers.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in community, enrolled in Medicare/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all Medicare/Medicaid services plus extras; no additional premiums for dual eligibles.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries to improve food security. It frees up income for other senior needs like care costs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with gross income ≤130% FPL (~$1,730/individual), net income test, assets ≤$3,750 (non-vehicle).
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household (varies by income/size).

programs.name: Low-Income Energy Assistance Program (LEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LEAP provides grants to help low-income households pay winter heating bills. It covers a portion of energy costs from November to April.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ in households with income ≤60% state median (~$3,200/year individual), receiving energy bills.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility bills. Services include professional assessments and installations.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (≤200% FPL) including seniors, priority for elderly/disabled/veterans.
programs.benefit_value: Free services; average savings $300+/year on energy bills.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare options, including Parts A/B/D, Medicare Advantage, and cost-saving programs. In Colorado, it's called Health Insurance Counseling and Advocacy Program.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries, caregivers, and family members aged 65+.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save average $1,000+/year on premiums/coverage.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels Colorado
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Provides hot meals weekdays plus frozen weekend meals.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or with mobility issues.
programs.benefit_value: 5 meals/week at suggested $3-5/donation (often free/subsidized for low-income); nutrition value ~$10/meal.

programs.name: Community Access Services (CAS) and Respite Care
programs.what_it_does: CAS offers one-time grants for home repairs, utilities, and adaptive equipment; respite provides temporary relief for caregivers. Supports aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤200% FPL; family caregivers of seniors/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,200/year grants; respite hours vary (paid ~$15-20/hour).

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP provides part-time paid training jobs in community organizations to help unemployed low-income seniors gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment. Includes support services.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed seniors 55+ with income ≤125% FPL, priority for hardest-to-serve.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/week, ~$8,000/year); training leading to full-time jobs.

programs.name: Colorado Legal Services (Legal Aid for Seniors)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free civil legal assistance for issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Includes advice, representation, and senior-specific clinics.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (income ≤125-200% FPL depending on case), priority for vulnerable.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services; resolves issues preserving ~$5,000+ in benefits/housing annually per client.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care and rights protection. Provides education and support.
programs.who_its_for: Residents/potential residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities; families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints; improves care quality and resident satisfaction.

programs.name: Old Age Pension (OAP)
programs.what_it_does: The Colorado Old Age Pension (OAP) is a state-funded cash assistance program providing up to $952 per month (as of 2023) to ensure a minimum income level for eligible seniors, reduced dollar-for-dollar by other income sources. It also offers limited medical benefits for those not qualifying for other coverage.
programs.who_its_for: Colorado residents aged 60+, with income below the maximum benefit, resources under $2,000 (individual) or $3,000 (couple), who have pursued all other benefits like Social Security or SSI.
programs.whats_unique: OAP is enshrined in the Colorado Constitution since 1937, predating federal SSI, making it a unique state-funded safety-net cash program not replicated in most states.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $952/month cash (2023); varies by income.

programs.name: Home Care Allowance (HCA)
programs.what_it_does: Part of Colorado's Medicaid HCBS waivers, HCA pays for personal care, respite, home modifications, and supplies to support aging in place. It enables seniors to receive home-based services instead of institutional care.
programs.who_its_for: Age 65+, income ≤300% Federal Benefit Rate (~$3,200/month individual), assets ≤$2,000.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific Medicaid waiver program under Health First Colorado, with defined payment caps tailored to Colorado's elder care needs, beyond standard federal Medicaid.

programs.name: Older Coloradans Cash Fund
programs.what_it_does: Provides one-time grants to low-income seniors for home repairs like ramps and grab bars, utility assistance, and minor adaptive equipment such as walkers or shower chairs. It addresses immediate safety and accessibility needs in the home.
programs.who_its_for: Age 60+, household income ≤200% Federal Poverty Level.
programs.whats_unique: Colorado-specific fund offering targeted one-time grants for elder home adaptations, not a standard federal program and unique to the state's elder support structure.
